element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Single Puyo Puyo mode teaches fundamentals through play
Demonstrated by AI opponents before player must master
Required to beat advanced opponents and Dark Prince

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Introduced in Puyo Puyo 2 (not in original 1992 Genesis version)

typical_tension_triggers: Incoming garbage blocks from opponent chains
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating pressure: early matches allow measured play; mid-story matches introduce rapid garbage sequences; final boss (Dark Prince) demands sustained high-speed chains and perfect offsetting positioning

difficulty_curve.early_game: First 3-4 opponents teach basic matching and chain concepts; puyos fall predictably, chains are telegraphed
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Opponents 5-10 introduce rapid garbage sequences and require player to build defensive chains; grid management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Opponents 11-12 and Dark Prince demand mastery of multi-chain sequencing, perfect timing, and rapid response to garbage block bursts
notable_spikes: Transition from single-chain to multi-chain opponents (mid-story)
Dark Prince final battle (requires sustained high-speed play and chain prediction)

notable_level_patterns.description: 12 predetermined opponents in order before Dark Prince; each opponent has characteristic play style and chain speed; player battles as Arle Nadja
Continuous gameplay with increasing puyo spawn rate and chain complexity; no fixed end state, pure score pursuit
52 individual missions with predefined board states; player must eliminate specific patterns using limited piece drops
Two players on identical split grids; garbage blocks sent to opponent based on chain quality; victory by opponent grid overflow
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Double Puyo Puyo (vs human or CPU)
